mechanisms.location: Settings > Extras > API Keys in Mailchimp
mechanisms.usage: HTTP Basic Auth with username 'anystring' and password 'apikey', or Bearer token
mechanisms.data_center: Embedded in API key (e.g., us6) — determines base URL
mechanisms.notes: Tied to the user who created it. If user is removed, API key is revoked. Check user role via API Root endpoint.

mechanisms.access_token_lifetime: Does NOT expire — no refresh token needed
mechanisms.notes: Access token remains valid unless user revokes app. Get server prefix (dc) from metadata endpoint after token exchange. Use OAuth in Authorization header.

authentication.user_role_restrictions: User role (admin, manager, etc.) limits what API actions are allowed. 403 returned if role lacks permission.

rate_limits.error_on_exceed: HTTP 429 (or 403 without JSON body at very high volumes)

batch_endpoint.description: For long-running or large operations — bypasses 10-connection limit
batch_endpoint.results_available: 7 days; download URL valid for 10 minutes
batch_endpoint.notes: Operations run async on Mailchimp servers

rate_limits.no_option_to_raise: Currently no option to raise limit per customer

core_endpoints.description: API Root — get account info, user role, and API version

webhooks.max_per_list: Not explicitly documented but batch webhooks max at 20
webhooks.hmac_signing: Supported for batch webhooks — verify signature against raw request body bytes

common_causes: Member Exists — using POST create instead of PUT upsert
error_dictionary.solution: Use PUT /3.0/lists/{list_id}/members/{subscriber_hash} for upsert (idempotent create/update). Pre-create merge fields matching HubSpot property names. Validate email format before submission

error_dictionary.description: User role lacks permission for the action or API access disabled
causes: User role (e.g., Viewer) cannot create campaigns or manage lists
API key invalid or user removed from account
error_dictionary.solution: Use an admin-level user for API operations. Check role via GET /3.0/ endpoint

error_dictionary.description: Resource does not exist (e.g., invalid list_id or subscriber hash)
error_dictionary.solution: Verify list_id and subscriber hash (MD5 of lowercase email). List IDs can be fetched from GET /3.0/lists

error_dictionary.description: Exceeded 10 simultaneous connections or high volume threshold
error_dictionary.real_response: At exceptionally high volumes, you may receive an HTTP 429 or 403 without a JSON body.
error_dictionary.solution: Implement request queuing — reduce parallelism. Use batch endpoint for large operations. 120s timeout means long requests should use batches

error_dictionary.description: Attempting to create a member that already exists using POST instead of PUT
error_dictionary.real_response: {"type":"http://developer.mailchimp.com/documentation/mailchimp/reference/overview/errors/","title":"Member Exists","status":400,"detail":"[email] is already a list member. Use PUT to insert or update list members."}
error_dictionary.solution: Switch from POST to PUT /3.0/lists/{list_id}/members/{subscriber_hash}. PUT is idempotent and handles both create and update

community_constraints.issue: Merge field case-sensitivity and format mismatch
community_constraints.detail: Mailchimp merge tags have character limits and formatting rules. A phone number stored as +63 917 123 4567 in CRM will fail silently when pushed to a 10-digit merge tag
community_constraints.workaround: Pre-create merge fields matching source system format. Validate and sanitize data formats before sync. Test with a test segment first

community_constraints.issue: Unsubscribe sync breaks — HubSpot keeps emailing unsubscribed contacts
community_constraints.detail: When a contact unsubscribes in Mailchimp, the native integration doesn't sync that status back to HubSpot automatically
community_constraints.workaround: Implement Mailchimp webhook to catch unsubscribes and update a custom property in HubSpot (e.g., mailchimp_unsubscribed). Use middleware (Zapier, n8n, Make) for bidirectional unsubscribe sync

community_constraints.issue: 10 concurrent connection limit is too restrictive for large syncs
community_constraints.detail: Bulk operations (e.g., syncing 50k contacts) hit the 10-connection ceiling quickly
community_constraints.workaround: Use Batch endpoint for async operations. Implement client-side queuing with max 10 concurrent requests

community_constraints.issue: Server prefix (dc) confusion in API URL construction
community_constraints.detail: API base URL must use the data center prefix (us1, us6, eu1, etc.) extracted from the API key or OAuth metadata. Using wrong prefix causes 404 or 403
community_constraints.workaround: For OAuth: always call metadata endpoint after token exchange. For API keys: parse the dc from the key suffix (e.g., key-us6 → dc=us6)

use_cases: Sync CRM contacts to Mailchimp audience for email campaigns
Trigger automations based on external events via API
Sync unsubscribe status bidirectionally between CRM and Mailchimp
Batch import large contact lists using the async Batch endpoint
Segment audiences based on CRM properties using merge fields
